description: I had pished in several Warbling Vireos (and a couple Wilson's Warblers) when I caught a glimpse of a bird washed yellow beneath, much the color of many fall Yellow Warblers. When it popped into view again, I could see that the bird was a Warbling/RE type vireo, washed from throat to vent in pale to medium clear yellow (no green hues), shorter tailed and billed than the nearby WAVIs. As I followed it through the trees (it circled me as I did a E Screech Owl whistle), I could eventually see a bright green back, gray crown, and black line through the eyes with subdued pale arc below eyes (c/w WAVI). The bird also had a moderate whitish supercilium. No wingbars.The yellow beneath was even, no brighter on flanks than mid chest or throat, in all lighting, crappy and decent. The bird was 20-30 feet from me while being observed
